rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes depending on the scope of the assumptions, guarantees, and bindings being declared.
Implementation-level signal routing bindings should be placed in the implementation scope after relevant subcomponents and connections are declared.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports should be placed in component type scopes, while implementation-level bindings belong in implementation scopes.
AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and should be placed in appropriate AADL component scopes.
Visible outputs in component type contracts must be related to visible inputs; implementation-only paths should not be used in type-level annexes.
Assignments in AGREE are used for implementation-level bindings and must be valid within the scope of the component implementation.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` statements within `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
Scalar type name resolution in AGREE
Type mismatch in assumed vs actual types
